sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the images, clicking on the empty gray cell at (13, 18) didn't seem to change anything visible. The green objects are still in the same positions. The G selection frame is still on (19, 18).
Let me check what actually changed by comparing frames. The step counter changed from 7 to 8, but I need to see if any gameplay objects changed.
Let me try a different approach:
1. Maybe I need to click on the green object itself, then use directional keys to move it
2. Maybe ACTION7 is the "execute" or "confirm" button
3. Maybe I need to click on empty cells in a specific order
Let me try ACTION7 to see what it does.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Check what changed from the last action
# Compare previous_frame to current_frame
if previous_frame:
    prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation
    curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation
    
    prev_nodes = {n['color']: n for n in prev_seg['nodes']}
    curr_nodes = {n['color']: n for n in curr_seg['nodes']}
    
    print("Previous frame colors:", [n['color'] for n in prev_seg['nodes']])
    print("Current frame colors:", [n['color'] for n in curr_seg['nodes']])
    
    # Check for G frame
    g_prev = [n for n in prev_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    g_curr = [n for n in curr_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    print(f"G frames: prev={len(g_prev)}, curr={len(g_curr)}")
    if g_curr:
        b = g_curr[0]['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  G center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2}, {(min(cols)+max(cols))//2})")

# Clicking on empty cell didn't change anything visible.
# Let me try ACTION7 to see what it does
action(['ACTION7'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
